CONTACT INFORMATION
anni.suoknuuti@helsinki.fiCONCLUSIONS
MEK inhibitors were the most commonly used targeted therapy and showed the highest response rates in low -grade serous ovarian ca rcinoma, 
where tumors shared mutation -independent MAPK -driven transcriptional programs, while poor response and targetable mutations were
associated with enhanced inflammatory and interferon -related gene expression.INTRODUCTION
•FINPROVE (NCT05159245) is Finland’s first multi -center 
phase II clinical trial advancing precision cancer medicine. 
•It evaluates the efficacy of EMA/FDA -approved targeted 
therapies based on tumor molecular profiles in patients who 
have exhausted standard treatments. 
•Despite shared genomic alterations, treatment responses in 
gynecological cancers vary, highlighting the need for deeper 
molecular insights. 
•Aim: To characterize the clinical, genomic, and 
transcriptomic features of gynecological cancer patients in 
FINPROVE and identify molecular characteristics 
associated with response to MEK -inhibitor therapy.METHODS
•From 1/2022 to 1/2026, 946 tumors were sequenced, including 131 (14%) 
gynecological cancers.
•A subset of tumors were analyzed with the NanoString pancancer IO 360 panel (770 
genes + 55 extra genes) and tissue cyclic immunofluorescence ( tCycIF , 25 antibodies)
1.Clinical & genomic characterization

most common tumour histology.3. The most frequently altered gene among gynecological
cancer patients was TP53 , followed by KRAS and PIK3CA.
4. TP53 mutations associated with CCNE1 and MYC amplification.
5. Among gynecological cancer patients in FINPROVE, MEK 
inhibitors were the most commonly administered targeted therapy.6. Positive responses to MEK -inhibitors were most frequent among patients with low -grade 
serous ovarian carcinoma.

presence of MAPK activating mutations.2. High PC1 is associated with immune activation, and

6. Poor response is characterized by increased expression of 
interferon -related genes ( IFITs, IFI6, RSAD2 ).5. Tumours with targetable mutations show distinct 
immune -and growth factor –related gene expression.7. LGSC tumours with targetable mutations exhibit 
inflammatory signalling.8. Within LGSC, poor response is associated with 
reduced expression of immune -related genes (CD1C, 
IL1R2).
